decks.notes: This Mono Green Aggro deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with Llanowar Elves and utilizing creature buffs to overpower opponents. Badgermole Cub and Sazh's Chocobo provide early aggression, while Earthbender Ascension and Mightform Harmonizer boost the attacking power of creatures. Esper Origins offers card advantage, while Mossborn Hydra serves as a late-game threat. The deck also includes versatile spells like Royal Treatment and Meltstrider's Resolve for removal and protection. The strategy revolves around overwhelming the opponent with large creatures and efficient use of mana resources.

The deck aims to control the game by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to gain card advantage and outvalue the opponent. It utilizes a mix of instants, sorceries, and enchantments to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The key strategy is to maintain board presence and card advantage while disrupting the opponent's threats, eventually leading to a win through attrition. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the deck's color requirements and ensure consistency in casting spells.

The main focus of this deck is to establish control over the game through a combination of efficient removal spells and counter cards. By utilizing cards like Burst Lightning, Traumatic Critique, Spell Snare, and Spell Pierce, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and prevent them from building a strong board presence. Eddymurk Crab and Hearth Elemental provide early game board presence, while cards like Opt and Sleight of Hand help to dig for key pieces of the control strategy. Overall, the deck relies on a mix of removal, counterspells, and card draw to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with key creatures and using powerful reanimation spells to bring them back onto the battlefield for a game-winning combo. It also includes various removal spells and hand disruption to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements and provide flexibility in casting spells. The strategy revolves around setting up the perfect conditions for a big reanimation play that can swing the game in your favor.

The main focus of this Azorius Aggro deck is to build a strong board presence through a combination of flying creatures and support cards. By utilizing efficient creatures like Momo, Friendly Flier and Sage of the Skies, along with cards like Springleaf Drum and Starfield Shepherd, this deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Quantum Riddler and Daydream provide disruption and card advantage to help maintain momentum. Overall, this deck aims to apply pressure early and consistently until victory is achieved.

The main concept of this deck revolves around utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to apply early pressure on the opponent while maintaining card advantage through various draw spells. The deck aims to quickly build a board presence with efficient creatures like Eddymurk Crab and Slickshot Show-Off, backed up by burn spells such as Burst Lightning and Prismari Charm to control the board. The inclusion of bounce spells like Boomerang Basics and Roaring Furnace/Steaming Sauna provides additional tempo advantage, allowing the deck to maintain pressure on the opponent and close out the game swiftly.

The deck focuses on slowing down the opponent's strategy through counterspells, removal, and card draw while maintaining control of the battlefield. Eddymurk Crab and Sunderflock provide early game defense, while Hearth Elemental and Colorstorm Stallion offer additional threats. Burst Lightning offers removal, while Opt, Prismari Charm, and Traumatic Critique provide card advantage and control options. Spell Snare and Spell Pierce help to stop key spells, while Three Steps Ahead and Winternight Stories offer additional card filtering. The mana base is carefully constructed to provide the necessary colors for spells and minimize the risk of mana issues.

The main concept of this deck is to establish a strong board presence quickly and overwhelm the opponent with a relentless stream of creatures. By utilizing powerful early game creatures like Badgermole Cub and Llanowar Elves, combined with efficient removal spells like Earthbender Ascension and Origin of Metalbending, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Escape Tunnel and Promising Vein also provide additional value and flexibility in maintaining momentum throughout the match.

This deck focuses on using a combination of creatures and spells to create a powerful synergy that can overwhelm the opponent quickly. By utilizing cards that support each other and enable multiple combos, the goal is to establish board control and maintain pressure on the opponent. A mix of aggressive creatures and utility spells allows for flexibility in playstyle and adaptability to different matchups. The deck aims to efficiently utilize resources and capitalize on key synergies to create advantageous board states and secure victory.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources and board state through a combination of removal spells, discard effects, and powerful threats like Mosswood Dreadknight and Scavenger Regent. The main goal is to disrupt the opponent's game plan and establish a dominant position on the battlefield, eventually overwhelming them with card advantage and powerful creatures. The inclusion of cards like Deadly Cover-Up and Withering Curse allows the player to efficiently deal with threats while generating advantages, while also utilizing lands like Unholy Annex and Ritual Chamber to ramp up and fix the mana base.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw. Eddymurk Crabs and Gran-Gran provide early defense, while spells like Accumulate Wisdom and Firebending Lesson help dig for answers. Combustion Technique and Boomerang Basics offer removal options, while Stormchaser's Talent can quickly close out the game. Multiversal Passage and Riverpyre Verge ensure consistent mana production, while Iroh's Demonstration and Impractical Joke offer additional utility. By maintaining board control and card advantage, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver its opponents.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of spells and abilities to manage the opponent's threats and disrupt their game plan. By utilizing a mix of counterspells, removal, and card draw, the deck can outlast opponents and gain incremental advantage over time. Additionally, the deck aims to generate card advantage through effects like Jeskai Revelation and Stormchaser's Talent, while also using versatile cards like Abrade and Ral, Crackling Wit to deal with a variety of threats. The deck's mana base is also carefully constructed to ensure consistency in casting spells and managing resources effectively.

The main focus of this Jeskai Control deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. By using cards like Resonating Lute and Consult the Star Charts to draw cards and set up for future turns, along with removal spells like Lightning Helix and Day of Judgment to deal with threats, the deck aims to outlast opponents and win through incremental advantage. The deck also includes cards like No More Lies and Jeskai Revelation to generate card advantage, allowing the player to outvalue opponents in the long run.

The main concept of this Jeskai Control deck is to control the game through card advantage, removal spells, and counterspells. The deck focuses on drawing cards with spells like Consult the Star Charts and Jeskai Revelation, while also dealing damage and gaining life with Lightning Helix. No More Lies and Stock Up help maintain card advantage, while cards like Spell Pierce and Spell Snare disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base includes various lands to ensure consistent mana availability for all three colors in the Jeskai color pie.

The main idea behind this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy using a combination of efficient creatures and spells. By utilizing cards like Badgermole Cub, Mossborn Hydra, and Sazh's Chocobo, the deck aims to establish a strong board presence early in the game. Additionally, cards like Earthbender Ascension and Mightform Harmonizer provide support and buffs to further increase the deck's offensive capabilities. With a mix of ramp, removal, and powerful finishers, this Selesnya Aggro deck is designed to put immense pressure on the opponent and secure victory through swift and decisive attacks.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures using cards like Deceit and Superior Spider-Man, and then reanimate them using cards like Bitter Triumph and Requiting Hex. The deck also includes disruption and protection cards like Deadly Cover-Up and Day of Black Sun to control the board and protect your combo pieces. With a mix of graveyard manipulation, reanimation, and control elements, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and outplay them strategically.
